name: Freight & Logistics
description: Rate enquiry to booked shipment for a freight forwarder — lanes, modes and Incoterms on the deal, plus a Shipments object tracking ETD and ETA.
ai_context: You are a CRM assistant for a freight forwarder / logistics provider. Contacts are the people at a shipper or consignee who book and pay for freight; a deal is a rate enquiry for a lane that ends either booked or lost, while the Shipment object tracks the job once it is moving. Rates, surcharges and transit times expire, so validity dates matter as much as the number. Never invent rates, sailing schedules, container numbers, customs classifications or ETAs — quote only what is recorded, and say when a rate has expired.

automation_rules.name: Qualify a new shipper before quoting
automation_rules.description: Quoting blind wastes a day of ops time. This raises the qualification call and lists what has to be known before a rate goes out.

params.title: New freight enquiry — qualification checklist
params.body: Before quoting {{contact.first_name}} {{contact.last_name}}, confirm: origin and destination door or port, mode, commodity and HS code, weight and dimensions or container type, Incoterm, monthly volume, required transit time, who they use today and why they are looking, and whether credit terms or prepaid.

automation_rules.name: Re-check rate validity when a deal moves stage
automation_rules.description: Ocean and air rates, surcharges and space allocations expire on their own schedule. Every stage move raises a task to re-confirm the quote is still the quote.

params.title: Confirm rate validity, surcharges and space allocation on {{deal.title}}

automation_rules.name: Close out a delivered shipment
automation_rules.description: Fires the moment a deal reaches Booking Confirmed — the commercial win, which is when the handover has to happen.

params.title: Confirm the POD is received and check {{contact.first_name}} is happy with the lane

kb_templates.company:
## About us

We are a [freight forwarder / NVOCC / 3PL] headquartered in [city], moving [modes] on [main trade lanes].

Replace this with your licences and accreditations — FMC/OTI or NVOCC number, customs broker licence, AEO or C-TPAT status, IATA cargo agent code, and your cargo liability cover.

## Network

Own offices in [cities]; partner agents elsewhere. Say which lanes we control end to end and which rely on an agent — it changes what we can promise.
kb_templates.products:
## Services

- **Ocean** — FCL and LCL, [named lanes], typical transit [days]
- **Air** — consolidations and express, [gateways]
- **Road** — FTL and LTL across [region]
- **Customs brokerage** — import and export entries, duty and tax
- **Warehousing and distribution** — [locations], pick and pack, cross-dock

## Charges to quote explicitly

Freight, origin and destination THC, documentation, customs entry, ISF or ENS filing, demurrage and detention free time, and fuel and security surcharges. Silence on any of these is where disputed invoices come from.
kb_templates.playbook:
## Qualifying a shipper

1. Lane: origin and destination, door or port, and which side controls the freight.
2. Commodity, HS code, and whether it is hazardous, temperature-controlled or oversized.
3. Volume and frequency — one-off, or [x] containers a month?
4. Incoterm, because it decides whether the enquiry is even ours to quote.
5. Required transit time and any hard delivery date.
6. Who moves it today, and what specifically is wrong with that.

## Quoting rules

Always state a validity date and the free time on demurrage and detention. Run a credit check before the first shipment, not after the invoice.

## When something goes wrong

Rolled sailing, customs hold, damage: call the customer before they call us, with the new ETA and the options. That call is why shippers stay.
kb_templates.process:
## From rate enquiry to invoice

1. **Rate Enquiry** — lane, mode and commodity captured.
2. **Credit Check** — new accounts screened and terms set before any rate goes out.
3. **Quoting** — carrier and agent rates gathered, all-in cost built.
4. **Quote Sent** — quote issued with a validity date and named inclusions.
5. **Booking Confirmed** — space booked, job reference issued, shipment record created.
6. **Delivered & Invoiced** — proof of delivery received and the invoice raised.

Lost deals get the real reason — rate, transit time, space, or incumbent held it.
kb_templates.competitors:
## Who we lose to

Global forwarders on price at volume; digital platforms on instant quotes. Replace with the forwarders you actually meet in bids, and what each one does better.

## Where we win

Controlled lanes, a named person who answers the phone, and honest ETAs when a sailing rolls. Fill in the two or three lanes where we are genuinely the better option — and be honest about the ones where we are not.
